channel.title: RoundSignal - Weekly funded-startup signals
channel.description: Each week: freshly-funded startups, the role to contact, and a ready-to-send angle.

item.title: RoundSignal 2026-W29: 10 freshly-funded accounts to sell to this week
item.description:
RoundSignal Issue 2026-W29

Freshly-funded B2B SaaS & tech startups (pre-seed -> Series B)

10 trigger-ready accounts, scored and contextualized.

Alta

heat 82

Trigger: $25M Series A (Google News / calcalistech.com, as of 08 Jul 2026); positions itself as 'AWS for marketing and sales'

Why now: Fresh Series A in the marketing/sales tech space means they're staffing up GTM and building out product - prime moment to sell RevOps, growth/marketing, or design services before they lock in vendors.

Who to contact: VP Marketing / Head of Growth

Angle: Reference their 'AWS for marketing and sales' framing and offer to be the GTM execution partner that helps them prove the analogy with real pipeline in the first 90 days.

source

Marker

heat 80

Trigger: $13M seed, emerged from stealth (Tech.eu, as of 09 Jul 2026); London-based AI writing product, co-founded by ex-DeepMind creative lead

Why now: Just out of stealth with $13M - they need brand, positioning, launch PR and early growth motion fast. A well-timed offer lands before they've built an internal team.

Who to contact: Co-founder / CEO

Angle: Congratulate them on emerging from stealth and pitch a launch-week PR + positioning sprint tailored to the ex-DeepMind founder story.

source

EdVisorly

heat 78

Trigger: $13.3M Series A (Crunchbase News, as of 08 Jul 2026); LA-based AI edtech automating college transfer/admissions workflows

Why now: A Series A B2B SaaS scaling an AI-native platform will be hiring and expanding sales/marketing - a clean fit for RevOps, recruiting, and growth service providers acting now.

Who to contact: Head of Revenue / COO

Angle: Reference their move to automate messy admissions back-office and offer RevOps tooling/process help so their sales ops scale as cleanly as their product.

source

Kord

heat 75

Trigger: £6.4M Series A (Tech.eu, as of 08 Jul 2026); UK fintech unifying onboarding, compliance and payments

Why now: Fresh Series A fintech scaling an end-to-end platform - they'll need compliance-savvy dev, design and growth help and likely fractional CFO support during a scaling phase.

Who to contact: CEO / Head of Product

Angle: Open with their onboarding+compliance+payments unification thesis and offer a design/dev sprint to reduce onboarding friction as they scale post-Series A.

source

Aria

heat 73

Trigger: €7M Series A extension + €240M debt facility (EU-Startups, as of 10 Jul 2026); Paris-based FinTech, embedded invoice financing

Why now: An equity extension plus a large debt facility signals aggressive scaling - RevOps, growth marketing and recruiting services are all in demand as they deploy capital.

Who to contact: Head of Growth / CMO · hello@getaria.com

Angle: Tie your pitch to Europe's late-payment crisis narrative and offer demand-gen support to accelerate merchant adoption of their financing platform.

source

Fleek

heat 72

Trigger: $25M Series B (Tech.eu, as of 08 Jul 2026); UK-based AI infrastructure for the secondhand clothing industry

Why now: Series B means they're scaling internationally and building teams - a strong window for recruiting, growth and RevOps providers before larger agencies get in.

Who to contact: VP Growth / Head of Operations

Angle: Reference their global B2B rollout for secondhand fashion and offer a growth/RevOps engagement to support new-market expansion post-Series B.

source

Sherpa

heat 70

Trigger: $2.2M pre-seed co-led by Seedcamp, DN Capital, Activant Capital (Tech.eu, corroborated by Google News, as of 09 Jul 2026); Germany-based AI OS for external workforce management

Why now: Multi-source coverage of a pre-seed with strong backers - early enough that they have no in-house marketing/design/recruiting, so fractional and freelance services are wide open.

Who to contact: Founder / CEO

Angle: Congratulate them on the Seedcamp-backed pre-seed and offer a lean brand + go-to-market foundation package built for a pre-seed budget.

source

Finto

heat 68

Trigger: $3.4M (YC-backed, Munich; Tech.eu, as of 08 Jul 2026); building AI agents for accounting

Why now: YC-backed and freshly funded AI accounting startup - they'll be building GTM from scratch, ideal for growth, PR and design freelancers to step in now.

Who to contact: Co-founder / CEO

Angle: Play up the 'chose Munich over the Valley' story and offer a European-market GTM/PR push that leverages that differentiated narrative.

source

Auxilius

heat 58

Trigger: €1.3M pre-seed (Tech.eu, as of 07 Jul 2026); automating enterprise compliance

Why now: (unverified) Early pre-seed with a single thin source - but a compliance SaaS at this stage needs branding, dev and early growth help, and there's little competition for their attention now.

Who to contact: Founder / CEO

Angle: Reference their enterprise-compliance automation angle and offer a lightweight design + messaging sprint to sharpen how they sell into enterprise buyers.

source

BidScript

heat 54

Trigger: €701k pre-seed (EU-Startups, as of 02 Jul 2026); tender-automation SaaS reporting up to 50% higher tender win rates

Why now: (unverified) Single-source pre-seed and slightly aging, but a B2B SaaS at pre-seed with a clear ROI story is a good target for growth and content services before their budget grows.

Who to contact: Founder / CEO

Angle: Lead with their 50%-higher-win-rate claim and propose a case-study-driven content/demand program to turn that stat into pipeline.

source

© RoundSignal. Signals sourced from public news; verify before outreach.
